person.name: Isabel de Clare

burialPlace.address: Guisborough, Redcar and Cleveland Unitary Authority, North Yorkshire, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Isabella de Clare (2 November 1226-10 July 1264) was the daughter of Gilbert de Clare, 4th Earl of Hertford and 5th Earl of Gloucester and Isabel Marshal. She is also known as Isabel de Clare, but this is however, the name of many women in her family.

«b»Family«/b»
Isabella's maternal grandparents were William Marshal, 1st Earl of Pembroke and his wife Isabel de Clare, 4th Countess of Pembroke. Isabella's paternal grandparents were Richard de Clare, 3rd Earl of Hertford and Amice FitzRobert.

Isabella was the fourth of six children, her brother was Richard de Clare, 6th Earl of Hertford. Her sister, Amice de Clare married Baldwin de Redvers, 6th Earl of Devon and was mother of Baldwin de Redvers, 7th Earl of Devon and Isabella de Fortibus, Countess of Devon.

«b»Marriage«/b»
Isabella was married on 12 May 1240 (at age thirteen and a half) to Robert de Brus, 5th Lord of Annandale. Isabella brought to him the village of Ripe, in Sussex. Her husband was a candidate to become King of Scotland, after the death of the young Margaret, Maid of Norway. Her husband did not however succeed; Robert's rival, John Balliol was elected King of Scotland in 1292.

«b»Robert and Isabella had up to six children:«/b»
1.) Robert (1243-1304)

2.) William, married Elizabeth de Sully, without issue

3.) Bernard, married firstly Alicia de Clare and married secondly Constance de Morleyn

4.) Richard (died before 26 January 1287)

5.) Isabella (1249-c. 1284), married (as his first wife) Sir John FitzMarmaduke, Isabel was buried at Easington, county Durham.

John Balliol's time as King of Scotland did not last long, he died in 1314. Isabella's grandson, Robert the Bruce became King of Scotland. Isabella did not however get to see this day, she died in 1264, aged thirty seven. Her husband married a second time, to Christina de Ireby, this marriage produced no children.

Despite claims to the contrary by amateur genealogists, there is no evidence that Isabella had other children.

sourceList.title: Isabel de Clare Le Brus, "Find A Grave Index"

zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Clare於1226年11月2日出生在加拿大卑詩省吉迪馬吉斯迪金吉迪馬吉斯迪金 C (帕特 1)武斯可時，她的父親Richard de Clare14歲，母親Isabel Marshal26歲。她與Sir Robert De Bruce V結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1280年享年54歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾雷卡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Clare was born on 2 November 1226, in Usk, Kitimat-Stikine C (Part 1), Kitimat-Stikine, British Columbia, Canada, her father, Richard de Clare, was 14 and her mother, Isabel Marshal, was 26. She married Sir Robert De Bruce V. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1280, at the age of 54, and was buried in Redcar, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Clare est née le 2 novembre 1226, à Usk, Kitimat-Stikine C (Part 1), Kitimat-Stikine, Colombie-Britannique, Canada, son père, Richard de Clare, avait 14 ans et sa mère, Isabel Marshal, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert De Bruce V. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1280, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterrée à Redcar, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Clare am 2. November 1226 in Usk, Kitimat-Stikine C (Part 1), Kitimat-Stikine, British Columbia, Kanada geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Clare 14 und ihre Mutter Isabel Marshal 26. Sie hat Sir Robert De Bruce V geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1280 im Alter von 54 Jahren und wurde in Redcar, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Clare è nata il il 2 novembre 1226 nel Usk, Kitimat-Stikine C (Part 1), Kitimat-Stikine, Columbia Britannica, Canada, sua padre, Richard de Clare, aveva 14 anni e sua madre, Isabel Marshal, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir Robert De Bruce V. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1280, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolta a Redcar, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Clareが1226年11月2日にカナダブリティッシュ コロンビア州キティマト-スティキンキティマト-スティキン ック (パート 1)ウスクで生まれた時，父Richard de Clareは14歳，母Isabel Marshalは26歳であった。Sir Robert De Bruce Vと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1280年に54歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアレドカーにて埋葬された。

ko.Usk, Monmouth, England: 캐나다 브리티시 컬럼비아 키팀앳-스티킨 키팀앳-스티킨 시 (파르트 1) 스크
ko.Tewkesbury Abbey, Gloucestershire, , Eng: 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Clare(이)가 1226년 11월 2일 캐나다 브리티시 컬럼비아 키팀앳-스티킨 키팀앳-스티킨 시 (파르트 1) 스크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Clare(은)는 14세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Marshal(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Sir Robert De Bruce V(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1280년 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 레드카르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Clare nasceu em 2 de novembro de 1226, em Usk, Kitimat-Stikine C (Part 1), Kitimat-Stikine, Colômbia Britânica, Canadá, seu pai, Richard de Clare, tinha 14 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Marshal, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Robert De Bruce V. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1280, com 54 anos, e foi sepultada em Redcar, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Tewkesbury Abbey, Gloucestershire, , Eng: тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Clare родилась 2 ноября 1226 г. в местности уск, китимат-стикаин к (парт 1), китимат-стикаин, Британская Колумбия, Канада, её отцу по имени Richard de Clare было 14 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Marshal, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert De Bruce V. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1280 г. в возрасте 54 лет и была похоронена в местности редкар, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
